recommendation: Recurring-pattern: add a matchable `cause` value for the class 'operating-doc / sibling prose contradicts the shipped implementation' to the combat-log cause enum (a Council-ratified governance edit; producers/judges never edit the enum). The grounded enum has three values (coverage-gap, design-overreach, spec-feature-contradiction) and fails closed off-enum, but a judge-iteration whose cause is 'the skill's own operating docs / a sibling design doc assert behavior the shipped impl no longer has' has no exact home — it is neither a missing scenario (coverage-gap) nor strictly spec.md-vs-.feature (spec-feature-contradiction). This class has now recurred: acaa41 seq1 flagged a design/loops.md sibling-prose contradiction, and plan-retirement-distill-gate's impl gate hit it again (SKILL.md/README.md framed the retirement gate as caller-judgment, contradicting the delivered mechanical engine) and could only be logged as spec-feature-contradiction by nearest-bucket fit, corrupting matchability. Recommend the Council ratify a distinct value (candidate: `prose-impl-contradiction`, superseding the never-ratified `sibling-prose-contradiction` name acaa41 used) so the doctrine loop can match this failure mode cleanly across missions.
